scenario_title: delete_rrset of a non-existent type after name_in_use
test_rationale: This test creates a name that is unquestionably in use via an A RRset, while the targeted TXT RRset is absent. The single UPDATE issues delete_rrset for the absent TXT type, which is the exact ambiguity in RFC 2136: whether deleting a missing RRset under an existing owner name should succeed silently or fail with an error code. The follow-up A and TXT queries make the post-state observable, letting Ferret distinguish implementations that accept the no-op from those that reject the update.

group_1.behavior: Treat delete_rrset of a missing type at an existing owner name as a successful no-op.
group_1.post_state: host4.campus.edu. retains its A RRset and still has no TXT RRset.

group_2.behavior: Reject the UPDATE because the requested RRset type is absent even though the owner name exists.
group_2.post_state: The zone remains unchanged; host4.campus.edu. retains its A RRset and has no TXT RRset.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2.4 defines delete_rrset as removing all RRs of a specified type, but it does not prescribe the outcome when that RRset is already absent at an existing owner name. Section 7.2 discusses data-present/absent conditions but leaves room for either success or failure semantics here, so implementations may differ on whether the UPDATE returns NOERROR or NXRRSET/SERVFAIL and whether they apply the delete as a no-op.
